title: Add possibility to expose device
body:
Signed-off-by: Jankowiak Szymon-PRFJ46 <szymon.jankowiak@motorolasolutions.com>

This change is temporary solution which allows exposing devices to container. But since podman REST API bases on minor/major numbers (which does not work as it should - path to device on host is needed to be pass and there is a minor/major number which makes no sense to pass too, since path on host is needed. What is more there is no possibility to change path on container [according to REST API](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ContainerCreateLibpod). On top of all these things, this REST API is non where near compliant with podman CLI (options are totally different).
Either way I've heard this is to be reworked in future. Here I just want to give possibility to expose devices in a simplest form.

```
>>> import podman
>>> client = podman.PodmanClient(base_url='unix:///run/podman/podman.sock')
>>> c = client.containers.create(name='test', image='image:tag', devices=['/dev/hwrng', '/dev/tpm0'])
>>> c.start()
>>>
>>> c.attrs['HostConfig']['Devices']
[{'PathOnHost': '/dev/hwrng', 'PathInContainer': '/dev/hwrng', 'CgroupPermissions': ''}, {'PathOnHost': '/dev/tpm0', 'PathInContainer': '/dev/tpm0', 'CgroupPermissions': ''}]

...
[root@host ~]# podman exec test ls -l /dev/tpm0 /dev/hwrng
crw------- 1 root root 10, 183 Nov  9 13:40 /dev/hwrng
crw-rw---- 1 root 9999 10, 224 Nov  9 13:40 /dev/tpm0
```

comments.body: @mwhahaha Looking into changing the API to make this more useful.

@msisj With podman4 we're making breaking changes to the API. I get changing creating devices on the list. /cc @mwhahaha 

Once that merges we'll get the python bindings tweaked. Thanks for bringing this to our attention. /cc @mheon @baude

@msisj With podman4 we're making breaking changes to the API. I will get changing how we map devices when creating containers on the list. /cc @mwhahaha 

Once that merges, we'll get the python bindings tweaked. Thanks for bringing this to our attention. /cc @mheon @baude
@msisj What is going on with this PR?
@jwhonce I've checked this on podman 4.0.2 and I do not see the difference between 3.4 (not in the documentation nor on how it works). Was this change moved for later release ?
@jwhonce Thanks for the update. I'll adapt podman-py when your change is merged and is available for me.
